stem: A factory produces steel beams using two sources. Mill A handles 78% of production with a known defect rate of 2.1%. Mill B handles 22% of production, but its defect rate is uncertain — recent quality audits suggest it lies between 3.3% and 6.2%, depending on raw material batch quality. A randomly selected steel beam is found to be defective. What is the probability it came from Mill B?

ground_truth.answer: P(Mill B | defective) ranges from 0.307 to 0.454 as Mill B's defect rate varies from 3.3% to 6.2%. Point estimate at 4.8%: 0.392.

solution_steps: Apply Bayes' theorem: P(B|def) = P(def|B)×P(B) / [P(def|A)×P(A) + P(def|B)×P(B)].
The interval [0.307, 0.454] captures the range of posterior probabilities.
common_failure_modes: Using only the midpoint defect rate and providing a single answer
Comparing defect rates directly without applying Bayes' theorem
